results.text_content: Late-night roadside inspectors in LAS-TNC logged a vehicle inspection audit after officers found missing reflective decals and an expired maintenance sticker during an after-hours stop. The audit packet references roadside photos, a mechanic attestation request, and a notice that the vehicle would remain unavailable for dispatch until the inspection remediation was confirmed.

A nighttime inspection finding in NYC-TLC recorded that an active driver was stopped after a brake-light complaint and then routed into a formal audit for incomplete inspection paperwork. Review notes mention odometer snapshots, portal upload gaps, and a compliance reminder that roadside findings can trigger temporary throttling while documents are reconciled.

An overnight roadside compliance stop in PAR-VTC produced an inspection audit after officers requested updated registration, mechanic sign-off, and proof that the latest safety checklist had been completed. The narrative ties the event to a narrow deadline for filing corrected paperwork and warns that repeat late-night findings can escalate to tier-2 enforcement review.
